gene_region.label: RET transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical RET genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window used by this app to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval begins.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: RET encodes RET, a receptor tyrosine kinase for GDNF-family neurotrophic signaling. This bundle emphasizes RET receptor kinase signaling, neural-crest development, MAPK/PI3K activation, endocrine tumor biology, and fusion oncology.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local RET bundle is RET receptor kinase biology oriented. Interpret markers through exact allele class, zygosity, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context rather than as stand-alone deterministic calls.
variant_effect_overview: RET variants can alter or tag RET receptor kinase signaling, neural-crest development, MAPK/PI3K activation, endocrine tumor biology, and fusion oncology; effect direction depends on allele class, tissue, dosage, and biological context.
Common markers, pharmacogenetic alleles, structural events, and high-impact pathogenic variants should be kept separate during interpretation.
condition_research_overview: MEN2 and medullary thyroid carcinoma review context
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: RET methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a RET receptor kinase signaling, neural-crest development, MAPK/PI3K activation, endocrine tumor biology, and fusion oncology gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for RET.
Because RET biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.

Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against RET receptor kinase signaling, neural-crest development, MAPK/PI3K activation, endocrine tumor biology, and fusion oncology.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use RET promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
Variant-plus-methylation review for RET receptor kinase signaling, neural-crest development, MAPK/PI3K activation, endocrine tumor biology, and fusion oncology.
MEN2 and medullary thyroid carcinoma review context

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000165731: RET coordinate source

biorender_visuals.label: RET biochemical figure starter
biorender_visuals.focus: RET: RET receptor kinase signaling, neural-crest development, MAPK/PI3K activation, endocrine tumor biology, and fusion oncology
biorender_visuals.template_title: Intracellular Layout - Receptor Signaling from Plasma Membrane to Nucleus (2)
biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a RET RET receptor kinase biology thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to RET receptor kinase signaling, neural-crest development, MAPK/PI3K activation, endocrine tumor biology, and fusion oncology and MEN2 and medullary thyroid carcinoma review context, Hirschsprung disease developmental context. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support.

variant_records.clinical_interpretation: RET MEN2 activating model is curated as a RET marker for MEN2 and medullary thyroid carcinoma review context, Hirschsprung disease developmental context. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the RET receptor kinase signaling, neural-crest development, MAPK/PI3K activation, endocrine tumor biology, and fusion oncology pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Curated research and clinical-context marker; external clinical classification is required for diagnostic use.
functional_effects: Can modify or tag RET receptor kinase signaling, neural-crest development, MAPK/PI3K activation, endocrine tumor biology, and fusion oncology.
Best interpreted with tissue, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context.
associated_conditions: MEN2 and medullary thyroid carcinoma review context
research_context: Use this marker as a RET pathway-context signal, not as a stand-alone diagnosis.
Sequence, methylation, expression, and protein data may point in different directions and should be reconciled.
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: Pair RET MEN2 activating model with RET methylation only as local regulatory context; methylation does not prove the variant's biochemical effect.

literature_findings.genotypes: RET MEN2 activating model or linked RET locus genotype states
